title: Master AI in Just 10 Hours: Google Launches Game-Changing AI Professional Certificate on Coursera
summary: Google has officially launched its first AI Professional Certificate, expanding its Generative AI catalog on Coursera, which has already attracted nearly three million enrollments globally. This new program is designed to help professionals integrate artificial intelligence into their daily work with practical, job-ready skills that go beyond basic prompting. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); As AI fluency rapidly becomes a baseline expectation across industries, this certificate positions learners to stay competitive in an evolving workforce. According to recent research conducted by Google and IPSOS, more than half of job postings requesting AI skills are now outside traditional IT and computer science roles. However, only 40% of American workers currently use AI at work. This certificate aims to close that gap. What Makes the Google AI Professional Certificate Unique? The Google AI Professional Certificate focuses on universal, transferable skills developed through analysis of hundreds of job descriptions and collaboration with employers. It emphasizes hands-on learning and responsible AI use. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Every learner who enrolls receives: Three months of no-cost access to Google AI Pro Practical experience using Google s advanced AI models A portfolio of real-world AI projects Additionally, Google is offering free access to the certificate for U.S. small businesses, alongside tools such as Google Workspace Business Standard to support AI adoption nationwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Program Structure and Courses The certificate can be completed in approximately 10 hours and includes seven short courses plus a hands-on capstone project. Courses include: AI Fundamentals Learn foundational generative AI concepts and responsible evaluation of AI outputs. AI for Brainstorming and Planning Use AI to generate structured project plans. AI for Research and Insights Synthesize information for faster decision-making. AI for Writing and Communication Craft persuasive and audience-specific messaging. AI for Content Creation Develop and critique multimedia assets responsibly. AI for Data Analysis Convert complex datasets into clear, actionable insights. AI for App Building Build a custom AI-powered solution using natural language. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Throughout the program, learners work with tools such as Gemini, NotebookLM, and AI Studio, gaining hands-on experience directly from Google experts. Skills You Will Gain By the end of the certificate, participants will have developed: Reusable AI prompts Data-driven project plans AI-generated research and visual content A custom AI-powered application Strong understanding of responsible AI practices ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The program is ideal for working professionals seeking to future-proof their careers and organizations aiming to upskill their teams. Deadline No official deadline has been announced. Enrollment is currently open. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity , click here. For more opportunities, click here. title: Launch Your Career in Civic Engagement: Apply for the CIVICUS Communications & Network Engagement Internship
summary: Are you passionate about civil society, citizen activism, and positive social change? CIVICUS, a global alliance of over 15,000 members across 189 countries, is looking for a Communications and Network Engagement Intern to join their team. This internship offers a unique opportunity to contribute to a worldwide mission defending people power and amplifying excluded voices, especially from the Global South. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); As a Communications and Network Engagement Intern, you will play a vital role in supporting CIVICUS global engagement through digital platforms. Your responsibilities will include creating compelling digital content, engaging with networks and membership, and collaborating with communicators across sectors to foster civic action. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys blending creativity, analytics, and relationship-building in a meaningful context. Key Responsibilities: Develop creative digital content for LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok. Strengthen network engagement with CIVICUS Narratives Network and members. Build relationships with communicators across civil society sectors. Support initiatives that amplify citizen activism and civic participation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Eligibility Requirements: Degree or equivalent experience in communications, marketing, social media, creative content development, or a related field. Excellent command of English; knowledge of a major Global South language is a plus. Experience in social media content creation, management, and analytics. Commitment to positive social change and civil society work. Strong interest in community engagement and civic action communications. How to Apply: Applications close 5th March 2026 at 11:59 PM SAST. Your application should include: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); A detailed CV including education, professional qualifications, significant positions, responsibilities, and achievements. A one-page cover note explaining your motivation for applying. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within two weeks of the closing date. All applicants will be updated once the position has been filled. CIVICUS reserves the right to withdraw any vacancy at any time. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); This internship is an excellent pathway for emerging communicators and social change makers looking to gain hands-on experience in global civic engagement and digital network strategy. Deadline : 5th March,2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more job opportunities, click here . title: EPIC Launches Brandt Research Fellowship and 2026–27 Pre-Doctoral Research Professional Roles in Climate & Energy Economics
summary: The Energy Policy Institute at the University of Chicago (EPIC) has announced a major expansion of its early-career research opportunities with the launch of the Brandt Research Fellowship , alongside multiple full-time pre-doctoral Research Professional positions for the 2026 27 academic cycle. These opportunities are designed to strengthen global research and policy impact in climate change, energy markets, and environmental economics. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The Brandt Research Fellowship is supported by a generous gift from Ted Brandt, MBA 87, founder and CEO of Marathon Capital, a clean-economy-focused advisory firm. The fellowship operates within EPIC s highly regarded Pre-Doctoral Fellows Program, which serves as a bridge between undergraduate or master s education and doctoral study. Brandt emphasized the importance of rigorous energy research in today s global economy, noting that energy economics and geopolitics influence nearly every sector. Through this fellowship, EPIC continues to ask critical policy questions and deliver evidence-based solutions that shape decision-making worldwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the EPIC Pre-Doctoral Fellows Program Brandt Research Fellows join EPIC for one or two years, working closely with EPIC-affiliated faculty and researchers. Fellows gain hands-on experience across all stages of the research process, including project management, data collection and analysis, manuscript preparation, and policy engagement. Research spans energy markets, climate impacts, environmental externalities, and public policy, often using quasi-experimental and experimental methods with large-scale global datasets. EPIC pre-doctoral alumni have gone on to top PhD programs worldwide, and several have received highly competitive National Science Foundation grants. Fellows leave the program with advanced technical skills, strong professional networks, and deep exposure to policy-relevant research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Research Focus and Methodology Research projects typically involve environmental and energy data from the United States, India, China, and other regions. Fellows apply advanced quantitative methods, computational modeling of energy markets, and policy evaluation techniques. In addition to research responsibilities, fellows participate in seminars and classes at EPIC, the University of Chicago , and partner institutions. Open Positions for the 2026 27 Academic Cycle EPIC is currently recruiting for multiple full-time, benefits-eligible Research Professional (Pre-Doctoral Fellow) roles beginning in Spring Summer 2026: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Research Professional Climate Impact Lab (Michael Greenstone) Fellows will work under the direction of Michael Greenstone , contributing to globally influential research that quantifies climate change impacts across sectors such as health, agriculture, labor, energy, conflict, migration, and coastal systems. This work supports the development of empirically derived estimates of the social cost of carbon. Salary range: $55,000 $62,000 annually Additional $2,000 professional development fund Duration: 1 2 years Expected start date: Spring Summer 2026 Applications reviewed on a rolling basis Research Professional Fiona Burlig, Erin Kelley Greg Lane This role focuses on experimental and data-intensive research in developing-country agriculture, climate adaptation, insurance markets, and climate risk forecasting, including randomized controlled trials in India. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Salary range: $55,000 $62,000 annually Additional $2,000 professional development fund Expected start date: July 1, 2026 Duration: 1 2 years APPLY HERE . For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more research fellowship opportunities here . Application Timeline and Deadlines Fall application cycle: Mid-November, with final decisions in mid-December Winter application cycle: Early March, with final decisions by late March ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Applicants must have completed a Bachelor s or Master s degree by June of the year of application and demonstrate strong quantitative and programming skills. EPIC offers a competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and an exceptional research environment that prepares fellows for top-tier doctoral programs. title: Alstom Foundation 2026 Call for Projects: Funding for NGOs Advancing Sustainable Development, Energy, Mobility & Environmental Protection
summary: The Alstom Foundation has officially announced the opening of its 2026 project cycle, providing funding opportunities for impactful NGO-led initiatives across key development sectors. The online submission portal opens on February 16, 2026, with a final submission deadline of April 10, 2026 (11:59 PM CET). This annual funding opportunity supports projects that drive meaningful change in communities where Alstom operates. NGOs partnering with an Alstom employee as Project Promoter are invited to submit proposals aligned with the Foundation s strategic priorities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Key Dates for the 2026 Cycle February 10 March 13, 2026: Project definition and preparation with partners March 13, 2026: Deadline to request assistance in identifying an Alstom Promoter February 16, 2026: Online submission portal opens April 10, 2026 (11:59 PM CET): Final deadline for project submission Mid-September 2026: Board selection of funded projects Implementation: Ongoing monitoring throughout the year Focus Areas of the Alstom Foundation The Alstom Foundation concentrates on four key axes of intervention: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); 1. Economic and Social Development Projects under this axis should promote sustainable livelihoods and inclusive growth. Priority is given to: Skills development and employability programs for disadvantaged youth and women Support initiatives for street children, orphans, and foster children ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); 2. Environmental Protection The Foundation funds projects that enhance environmental awareness, safeguard biodiversity, protect natural habitats, and strengthen community resilience to natural disasters. 3. Access to Mobility Proposals should facilitate safe and inclusive movement of people and goods, especially in remote or underserved communities, enabling better access to markets, healthcare, and education. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); 4. Access to Energy and Water Projects that improve access to electricity and safe drinking water are strongly encouraged. With millions globally lacking these essential services, the Foundation aims to raise living standards in vulnerable communities through sustainable solutions. Selection Criteria To be considered for funding, each project must: Align with at least one of the Foundation s key focus areas Serve the general interest Be located in a country where Alstom has a presence Demonstrate measurable outcomes and community impact Be realistic and feasible within budget and timeline Involve local stakeholders and local management Be implemented by a competent partner organization Comply with the Foundation s Ethics Charter Be submitted in the same country where the Alstom employee promoter is based Each NGO may submit only one project per cycle. The Role of the Alstom Promoter Every submission must be jointly prepared by an NGO and an Alstom employee serving as Project Promoter. The promoter must be based in the same country where the project will be implemented. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The promoter collaborates in preparing and validating the project file and, if selected, assists in monitoring implementation. If an NGO does not know an Alstom employee, it may request support from the Foundation Secretariat to identify a potential promoter. However, requests must be submitted no later than March 13, 2026. The Secretariat does not guarantee that a promoter will be identified, as promoters participate on a voluntary basis. How to Submit The online submission tool opens on February 16, 2026. Applicants complete structured, preformatted forms designed to optimize proposal clarity. A help function is available within each form. Drafts can be saved and edited before final submission. Once submitted, the selection process begins automatically. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Applicants will receive a confirmation email upon successful submission. Interested NGOs should begin early preparation to ensure compliance with all guidelines and partnership requirements before the April 10, 2026 deadline. Deadline : April 10 ,2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this funding opportunity, click here . For more grant opportunities, click here . title: Youth, Peace and Security Online Course 2026: UN-Certified Training to Implement the YPS Agenda
summary: Build Practical Skills to Advance Youth Participation in Peace and Security Young people are increasingly recognized as powerful agents of change in preventing conflict, sustaining peace, and shaping inclusive security policies. To translate global commitments into real-world impact, practitioners need the right skills, frameworks, and tools. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Realizing the Youth, Peace and Security Agenda is a comprehensive, practitioner-focused online course jointly developed by United Nations System Staff College and the Folke Bernadotte Academy . The programme equips professionals working in peace and security with the knowledge and applied competencies needed to effectively implement the Youth, Peace and Security (YPS) agenda while working with and for young people. Why This Course Matters The adoption of landmark UN Security Council resolutions 2250 (2015), 2419 (2018), and 2535 (2020) elevated youth participation in peace and security from aspiration to international obligation. Despite progress, implementation remains uneven across local, national, and global levels. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This course responds directly to that gap by offering a structured, skills-based learning journey that strengthens institutional capacity and supports young women and men as contributors to peaceful and just societies. Course Overview Language: English Format: Fully online Duration: Approximately 2 months of active learning (spread across 2026) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Weekly workload: 3 4 hours Course fee: USD 1,250 Enrollment Deadline: 10 April 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . The programme blends self-paced modules, live expert-led webinars, peer exchange, and written assignments. Participants enjoy flexible access via the UNSSC online learning platform, enabling learning from anywhere in the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Course Structure and Timeline Part I YPS Foundational Skills 13 April 22 May 2026 Module 1: Introduction to the Youth, Peace and Security Agenda Module 2: Enabling meaningful youth participation Module 3: Conducting youth-sensitive conflict analysis ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Includes one mandatory written assignment. Part II YPS Applied Skills (Elective Modules) 14 September 30 October 2026 Module 4: Serving as a YPS adviser or focal point Module 5: Youth-responsive and YPS-focused programming Module 6: In-country implementation of the YPS Agenda ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Includes at least one written assignment. Successful completion requires finishing all foundational modules and at least one elective module with assignments. Learning Methodology Participants engage in: Guided self-study modules Live interactive webinars (six in total) Peer learning and experience sharing Individual feedback and one-to-one facilitator support This approach ensures both conceptual understanding and practical application of YPS principles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Who Should Apply The course is designed for practitioners and representatives from: The UN system International and regional security organizations Non-governmental organizations Civil society organizations working on youth, peace, and security Selection Process Selection is competitive and based on: Quality of motivation statement Relevant experience and knowledge Commitment and availability Diversity across gender, geography, and sector Willingness to actively contribute to peer learning UN applicants must also submit a supervisor s endorsement as part of the online application. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Discover more learning opportunities here . title: Fully Funded PhD in Social & Cultural Anthropology (Health & Environment) – KU Leuven & Makerere University | 2026 Intake
summary: A prestigious opportunity is now open for aspiring anthropologists interested in youth mental health, gambling practices, and environmental crisis in East Africa. The Department of Social and Cultural Anthropology at KU Leuven, in collaboration with Makerere University, is offering one fully funded 3-year dual PhD fellowship under the EU-funded HEALENAE Doctoral Network supported by Marie Sk odowska-Curie Actions (MSCA-DN). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); This interdisciplinary doctoral project examines the intersection between youth mental health, gambling epidemics, and environmental insecurity in East Africa. Start Date: 1 September 2026 Location: Leuven, Belgium Kampala, Uganda (Dual Degree) About KU Leuven Founded in 1425, KU Leuven is one of Europe s leading research universities. The Department of Social and Cultural Anthropology hosts a vibrant research community of lecturers, postdoctoral researchers, and PhD candidates working across global themes of making, living, and moving. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The department emphasizes long-term ethnographic research to understand how individuals and communities navigate complex social, political, and environmental realities. About the HEALENAE Project HEALENAE (Health and Environment in Africa and Europe) is an EU Horizon Europe-funded doctoral network connecting universities across: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Aarhus (Denmark) Cape Town (South Africa) Edinburgh (UK) Leuven (Belgium) Nairobi (Kenya) Oslo (Norway) Makerere University (Uganda) The programme provides: Cross-continental anthropological training One full year of fieldwork in Africa and/or Europe Annual training schools and writing retreats A 6-month stay at the secondary university Attractive salary (living + mobility allowance + family allowance if applicable) Social security and pension benefits Funding for research, travel, conferences, books, and equipment ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Research Focus: Youth Mental Health Gambling in Times of Environmental Crisis This PhD project investigates how different forms of gambling (sports betting, crypto trading, online money games, lotteries, fortune-telling) intersect with environmental and socio-economic insecurity among youth in East Africa. Key Research Objectives Map offline and online gambling practices among youth Examine historical and cultural transformations of gambling and probability practices Study how environmental instability influences risk-taking behaviors Explore gambling as a perceived strategy for securing uncertain futures Analyze risk and uncertainty in post-colonial contexts Expected Impact The research will generate: In-depth gendered and locally grounded insights Better understanding of gambling s impact on youth mental health Knowledge for prevention of gambling addiction (ludomania) Policy-relevant insights into environmental crisis and youth vulnerability ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Planned Secondments One semester at Makerere University for supervision and academic exchange Potential engagement with NGOs such as Hope for Future Generations (or similar organizations in the field site) Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Hold an internationally recognized Master s degree (120 ECTS) in anthropology or related disciplines Graduate no later than 31 August 2026 Have less than 4 years of research experience after Master s Not hold a PhD degree Demonstrate strong academic performance Meet KU Leuven English language requirements Not have resided in Belgium for more than 12 of the last 36 months before recruitment Applicants of all nationalities are encouraged to apply. African language skills and non-academic experience are considered an asset. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Dual Degree Structure The selected candidate will: Be enrolled at both KU Leuven (Primary Institution) and Makerere University (Secondary Institution) Receive a dual PhD degree Undergo evaluation according to both universities regulations Defend publicly at KU Leuven and complete a viva at Makerere University Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Motivation letter (max 1 page, 2,400 characters) CV (education, positions, publications, activities) Research project proposal (max 12,000 characters excluding references timetable) including: Topic overview Research questions Literature discussion Methods and field sites Ethical considerations Bibliography Timetable Cover sheet Copies of academic diplomas and transcripts ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); All applications must be submitted in English. Deadline: 1 March 2026, 23:59 CET For more information click here Apply here discover scholarships opportunities here The post Fully Funded PhD in Social Cultural Anthropology (Health Environment) – KU Leuven Makerere University | 2026 Intake appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
